design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through side-scrolling levels as Simba, switching between cub form (jump-based enemy defeat) and adult form (combat moves), collecting bugs for health/meter upgrades, finding hidden bugs to unlock bonus Timon & Pumbaa mini-games
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Navigation hazards (bottomless pits, water, lava, falling obstacles) combined with enemy encounters and limited lives economy; resource scarcity around health/roar meter extensions creates strategic bug-hunting pressure
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience Simba's transformation from vulnerable cub to powerful adult lion warrior across iconic film moments, with Disney animator-quality sprite work grounding the fantasy in authentic character animation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game was intentionally made harder mid-development (monkey puzzle in 'Can't Wait to be King' expanded to meet Disney's rental-duration criteria); refuses to pad playtime with extra levels, instead deepening existing level difficulty; commits to life-loss permanence with clear fail-state (Game Over returns to title screen)

difficulty.lives_system: Variable starting lives based on difficulty setting selected; lives lost on health depletion, bottomless pit falls, water/lava contact, or environmental hazards (rolling boulders, falling stalactites). Certain difficulty modes grant more starting lives
difficulty.continues_system: Limited continues earned by collecting Circle of Life icons hidden throughout stages; continues allow restart from beginning of last stage on Game Over; if continues depleted, full reset to title screen required
difficulty.checkpointing: Checkpoints placed throughout levels allow restart from checkpoint location upon life loss; checkpoints do not replenish lives or health
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Difficulty setting chosen at game start determines starting lives; no mid-game difficulty adjustment available. Players have agency through optional bug-hunting for permanent meter extensions, bonus stage access for extra lives/continues, and checkpoint utilization strategy

progression.upgrade_system: Non-traditional: rare bug pickups permanently extend both health and roar meters for remainder of game (not permanent unlocks, but per-session enhancements). Standard bugs restore current health. Roar meter regenerates during play and can be consumed for puzzle-solving and enemy stunning
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression driven by level completion in sequence. Simba's moveset expands at narrative midpoint (cub→adult transformation), granting access to scratching, mauling, and throw attacks. Hidden bugs unlock access to bonus Timon & Pumbaa stages rather than new main-path content
replayability_hooks: Hidden bug locations enable bonus stage discovery on replays
Difficulty setting variation changes starting lives and challenge curve
Continue-gated stages require resource farming on subsequent attempts
Debug menu with level select available via cheat code (BARRY) for speedrunning/exploration

world_building_techniques: Level design based directly on iconic film locations (Pride Rock, Elephant Graveyard, Jungle, Wildebeest Stampede, etc.)
Narrative progression mirrors film arc: young Simba vulnerability→exile→adult return
Two levels (Hakuna Matata, Be Prepared) adapted from film scenes cut from final movie, expanding narrative scope
Sprite animation by Disney Feature Animation staff maintains character authenticity and visual storytelling continuity
Boss encounters implicitly tied to story beats (final battle with Scar as narrative climax)

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters function as narrative climax markers rather than puzzle-dependent gauntlets; tied to film story beats and character confrontations. Scar serves as final boss representing Simba's ultimate transformation and justice narrative

faction_indicators: Timon & Pumbaa (comic relief allies, neutral zone dwellers)
danger_signals: Rolling boulders during Wildebeest Stampede (environmental hazard signaling danger without agency)
Bottomless pits and water/lava boundaries (level design hazard boundaries)
Black spiders in bug collection (damage on contact, inverse of collectible reward)
environmental_storytelling: Pride Rock as safe origin space transitioning to Elephant Graveyard (hostile territory marker)
Jungle exile zone representing Simba's displacement and vulnerability
Stampede level as environmental narrative of danger and survival
Scar's lair (Be Prepared level) as villain stronghold with secret passages hinting at hidden history

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - The Lion King (SNES)
